# typed: false

class Vote < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :story
  belongs_to :comment, optional: true

  normalizes :reason, with: ->(r) { r.to_s }, apply_to_nil: true

  # for comment_vote_summaries
  attribute :count, :integer
  attribute :usernames, :string

  validates :vote, presence: true, inclusion: {in: [1, -1]}
  validates :reason,
    length: {is: 1},
    allow_blank: true,
    presence: true
  validate :validate_flagger_hasnt_replied

  scope :comments_flags, ->(comments, user = nil) {
    q = where(comment: comments, vote: -1)
    user ? q.where(user: user) : q.all
  }

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
  COMMENT_REASONS = {
    "O" => "Off-topic",
    "M" => "Me-too",
    "T" => "Troll",
    "U" => "Unkind",
    "S" => "Spam",
    "" => "Cancel"
  }.freeze
  ALL_COMMENT_REASONS = COMMENT_REASONS.merge({
    "I" => "Incorrect"
  }).freeze

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about
  STORY_REASONS = {
    "O" => "Off-topic",
    "A" => "Already Posted",
    "B" => "Broken Link",
    "S" => "Spam",
    "" => "Cancel"
  }.freeze
  ALL_STORY_REASONS = STORY_REASONS.merge({
    "Q" => "Low Quality"
  }).freeze

  def on_comment?
    comment_id.present?
  end

  def on_story?
    comment_id.blank?
  end

  def reason_text
    if on_story?
      ALL_STORY_REASONS[reason]
    else
      ALL_COMMENT_REASONS[reason]
    end
  end

  def self.comment_vote_summaries(comment_ids)
    Vote
      .joins(:user)
      .select("comment_id, reason, count(1) as count, group_concat(username separator ', ') as usernames")
      .where(comment_id: comment_ids)
      .where.not(reason: "")
      .group(:comment_id, :reason)
      .group_by(&:comment_id)
  end

  def self.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(user, stories)
    votes = {}

    Vote.where(user_id: user, story_id: stories,
      comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
      votes[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end

    votes
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_story_hash(user_id, story_id)
    votes = {}

    Vote.where(
      user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id
    ).where.not(comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
      votes[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end

    votes
  end

  def self.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(user_id, story_ids)
    if story_ids.empty?
      {}
    else
      votes = where(
        user_id: user_id,
        comment_id: nil,
        story_id: story_ids
      )
      votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
        memo[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
      end
    end
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(user_id, comment_ids)
    return {} if user_id.nil? || comment_ids.empty?

    votes = where(
      user_id: user_id,
      comment_id: comment_ids
    ).select(:comment_id, :vote, :reason)
    votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
      memo[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end
  end

  def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
    new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
  )
    v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
      comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize

    # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
    # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
    # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
    # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
    # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
    # happen with associated comments.
    v.story if story_id.present?

    return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change

    # score deltas when flags no longer affect scores
    # no vote  -> 1      1
    # flag -> 1          1
    # no vote  -> flag   0
    # flag -> no vote    0
    # 1  -> no vote     -1
    # 1  -> flag        -1
    score_delta = if new_vote == 1
      1
    elsif v.vote == 1
      -1
    else
      0
    end
    flag_delta = if v.vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag
      -1
    elsif new_vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
      1
    else
      # change from 1 to 0 or 0 to 1, so number of flags doesn't change
      0
    end

    if new_vote == 0
      v.destroy!
    else
      v.vote = new_vote
      v.reason = reason
      v.save!
    end

    if update_counters
      t = v.target
      if v.user_id != t.user_id
        User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta
      end
      t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    end
  end

  def target
    if comment_id
      Comment.find(comment_id)
    else
      Story.find(story_id)
    end
  end

  def validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
    if vote == -1 && comment_id && Comment.where(user_id: user_id, parent_comment_id: comment_id).exists?
      errors.add(:base, "You've already replied to the comment.")
    end
  end
end

class Vote < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :user
  belongs_to :story
  belongs_to :comment, optional: true

  normalizes :reason, with: ->(r) { r.to_s }, apply_to_nil: true

  # for comment_vote_summaries

  attribute :usernames, :string

  validates :reason,
    length: {is: 1},
    allow_blank: true,
    presence: true

    q = where(comment: comments, vote: -1)
    user ? q.where(user: user) : q.all

  # don't forget to edit the explanations on /about

    "O" => "Off-topic",

  def on_story?

  end

  def self.comment_vote_summaries(comment_ids)

      .where(comment_id: comment_ids)
      .where.not(reason: "")
      .group(:comment_id, :reason)

  def self.votes_by_user_for_stories_hash(user, stories)

    Vote.where(user_id: user, story_id: stories,

    end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_story_hash(user_id, story_id)
    votes = {}

    ).where.not(comment_id: nil).find_each do |v|
      votes[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end

    votes
  end

  def self.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(user_id, story_ids)
    if story_ids.empty?
      {}
    else
      votes = where(
        user_id: user_id,
        comment_id: nil,
        story_id: story_ids
      )
      votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
        memo[v.story_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
      end
    end
  end

  def self.comment_votes_by_user_for_comment_ids_hash(user_id, comment_ids)
    return {} if user_id.nil? || comment_ids.empty?

    votes = where(
      user_id: user_id,
      comment_id: comment_ids
    ).select(:comment_id, :vote, :reason)
    votes.each_with_object({}) do |v, memo|
      memo[v.comment_id] = {vote: v.vote, reason: v.reason}
    end
  end

  def self.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because(
    new_vote, story_id, comment_id, user_id, reason, update_counters = true
  )
    v = Vote.where(user_id: user_id, story_id: story_id,
      comment_id: comment_id).first_or_initialize

    # AR wraps a transaction around INSERTing to votes. I don't know why, but it also SELECTs the
    # associated story even though there isn't a touch: or callback on the association. This can
    # lead to a rare deadlock if someone is creating a story at the some time, because that has to
    # lock the stories table and then votes. This next line eagerly loads the associated story so
    # the SELECT doesn't happen during the transaction INSERTing to votes. Oddly, this doesn't
    # happen with associated comments.
    v.story if story_id.present?

    return if !v.new_record? && v.vote == new_vote # done if there's no change

    # score deltas when flags no longer affect scores
    # no vote  -> 1      1
    # flag -> 1          1
    # no vote  -> flag   0
    # flag -> no vote    0
    # 1  -> no vote     -1
    # 1  -> flag        -1
    score_delta = if new_vote == 1
      1
    elsif v.vote == 1
      -1
    else
      0
    end
    flag_delta = if v.vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be removing a flag
      -1
    elsif new_vote == -1
      # we know there's a change, so we must be adding a flag
      1
    else
      # change from 1 to 0 or 0 to 1, so number of flags doesn't change
      0
    end

    if new_vote == 0
      v.destroy!
    else
      v.vote = new_vote
      v.reason = reason
      v.save!
    end

      t = v.target

        User.update_counters t.user_id, karma: score_delta

    end
  end

    if comment_id
      Comment.find(comment_id)
    else
      Story.find(story_id)
    end
  end

  def validate_flagger_hasnt_replied
    if vote == -1 && comment_id && Comment.where(user_id: user_id, parent_comment_id: comment_id).exists?
      errors.add(:base, "You've already replied to the comment.")
    end
  end
end

339	  def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
340	    self.score += score_delta
341	    self.flags += flag_delta
342	    new_confidence = calculated_confidence
343	    # confidence_order allows sorting sibling comments by confidence in queries like story_threads.
344	    # confidence_order must sort in ascending order so that it's in the right order when
345	    # concatenated into confidence_order_path, which the database sorts lexiographically. It is 3
346	    # bytes wide. The first two bytes map confidence to a big-endian unsigned integer, inverted so
347	    # that high-confidence have low values. confidence is based on the number of upvotes and flags,
348	    # so some values (like the one for 1 vote, 0 flags) are very common, causing sibling comments to
349	    # tie. If we don't specify a tiebreaker, the database will return results in an arbitrary order,
350	    # which means sibling comments will swap positions on page reloads (infrequently and
351	    # intermittently, real fun to debug). So the third byte is the low byte of the comment id. Being
352	    # assigned sequentially, mostly the tiebreaker sorts earlier comments sooner. We average ~200
353	    # comments per weekday so seeing rollover between sibling comments is rare. Importantly, even
354	    # when it is 'wrong', it gives a stable sort.
355	    Comment.connection.execute <<~SQL
356	      UPDATE comments SET
357	        score = (select coalesce(sum(vote), 0) from votes where comment_id = comments.id),
358	        flags = (select count(*) from votes where comment_id = comments.id and vote = -1),
359	        confidence = #{new_confidence},
360	        confidence_order = concat(lpad(char(65535 - floor(#{new_confidence} * 65535) using binary), 2, '\0'), char(id & 0xff using binary))
361	      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
362	    SQL
363	  end

file.content:
def update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)
    self.score += score_delta
    self.flags += flag_delta
    new_confidence = calculated_confidence
    # confidence_order allows sorting sibling comments by confidence in queries like story_threads.
    # confidence_order must sort in ascending order so that it's in the right order when
    # concatenated into confidence_order_path, which the database sorts lexiographically. It is 3
    # bytes wide. The first two bytes map confidence to a big-endian unsigned integer, inverted so
    # that high-confidence have low values. confidence is based on the number of upvotes and flags,
    # so some values (like the one for 1 vote, 0 flags) are very common, causing sibling comments to
    # tie. If we don't specify a tiebreaker, the database will return results in an arbitrary order,
    # which means sibling comments will swap positions on page reloads (infrequently and
    # intermittently, real fun to debug). So the third byte is the low byte of the comment id. Being
    # assigned sequentially, mostly the tiebreaker sorts earlier comments sooner. We average ~200
    # comments per weekday so seeing rollover between sibling comments is rare. Importantly, even
    # when it is 'wrong', it gives a stable sort.
    Comment.connection.execute <<~SQL
      UPDATE comments SET
        score = (select coalesce(sum(vote), 0) from votes where comment_id = comments.id),
        flags = (select count(*) from votes where comment_id = comments.id and vote = -1),
        confidence = #{new_confidence},
        confidence_order = concat(lpad(char(65535 - floor(#{new_confidence} * 65535) using binary), 2, '\0'), char(id & 0xff using binary))
      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
    SQL
  end

  def gone_text
    if is_moderated?
      "Comment removed by moderator " <<
        moderation.try(:moderator).try(:username).to_s << ": " <<

700	            (vote = 1) or
701	            (
702	              vote = -1 and
703	              not exists ( -- user didn't comment
704	                select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
705	              )
706	            )
707	          )
708	        ),
709	        flags = (select count(*) from votes where story_id = stories.id and comment_id is null and
710	          vote = -1 and
711	          not exists ( -- user didn't comment
712	            select 1 from comments where votes.user_id = comments.user_id and comments.story_id = stories.id
713	          )
714	        ),
715	        hotness = #{calculated_hotness}
716	      WHERE id = #{id.to_i}
717	    SQL
718	  end

input.command: sed -n '232,242p' app/models/comment.rb; echo "---story calc---"; sed -n '550,560p' app/models/story.rb

content.content:
def calculated_confidence
    return 0 if is_deleted? || is_moderated?
    # matching the reddit implementation by backing out these numbers from how we cache
    ups = self.score + flags
    downs = flags
    n = BigDecimal(ups + downs) # the float version can accumulate enough error to go out of range
    return 0 if n == 0
    raise ArgumentError, "n should count number of upvotes + flags; that can't be a negative number" if n < 0

    z = BigDecimal("1.281551565545") # 80% confidence
    p = BigDecimal(ups) / n
---story calc---
  def calculated_hotness
    # take each tag's hotness modifier into effect, and give a slight bump to
    # stories submitted by the author
    base = tags.sum(:hotness_mod) + ((user_is_author? && url.present?) ? 0.25 : 0.0)

    # give a story's comment votes some weight, ignoring submitter's comments
    cpoints = if base < 0
      0
    else
      merged_comments.where.not(user_id: user_id).sum("comments.score + 1").to_f * 0.5
    end

tool_use_result.stdout:
def calculated_confidence
    return 0 if is_deleted? || is_moderated?
    # matching the reddit implementation by backing out these numbers from how we cache
    ups = self.score + flags
    downs = flags
    n = BigDecimal(ups + downs) # the float version can accumulate enough error to go out of range
    return 0 if n == 0
    raise ArgumentError, "n should count number of upvotes + flags; that can't be a negative number" if n < 0

    z = BigDecimal("1.281551565545") # 80% confidence
    p = BigDecimal(ups) / n
---story calc---
  def calculated_hotness
    # take each tag's hotness modifier into effect, and give a slight bump to
    # stories submitted by the author
    base = tags.sum(:hotness_mod) + ((user_is_author? && url.present?) ? 0.25 : 0.0)

    # give a story's comment votes some weight, ignoring submitter's comments
    cpoints = if base < 0
      0
    else
      merged_comments.where.not(user_id: user_id).sum("comments.score + 1").to_f * 0.5
    end

content.text:
I have a complete picture. Here is the summary.

## Findings

**Step 1 — Call site & target resolution**

Every cast vote routes through `Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because` (`app/models/vote.rb:127`). Inside it:
- Target record is resolved at `app/models/vote.rb:180` (`t = v.target`), and `Vote#target` is defined at `app/models/vote.rb:185-191` — returns `Comment.find(comment_id)` if `comment_id` present, else `Story.find(story_id)`.
- The recalculation is invoked at `app/models/vote.rb:181` (`t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta)`), guarded by `update_counters`.

**Step 2 — What it recomputes / persists**

There are two polymorphic implementations (same name, dispatched via the resolved target):

- `Story#update_score_and_recalculate!` — `app/models/story.rb:693-718`. Bumps in-memory `self.score`/`self.flags` (lines 694-695), then a raw `UPDATE stories` writes **`score`** (sum of upvotes + non-commenter flags), **`flags`** (count of non-commenter flag votes), and **`hotness`**. Hotness value comes from `calculated_hotness` (`app/models/story.rb:550`).
- `Comment#update_score_and_recalculate!` — `app/models/comment.rb:339-363`. Bumps in-memory `self.score`/`self.flags` (340-341), then a raw `UPDATE comments` writes **`score`**, **`flags`**, **`confidence`**, and **`confidence_order`**. Confidence value comes from `calculated_confidence` (`app/models/comment.rb:232`).

**Step 3 — Change map**

Edit targets:
- `app/models/story.rb:693-718` and `app/models/comment.rb:339-363` (the two method bodies).
- Their value helpers if scoring changes: `app/models/story.rb:550` (`calculated_hotness`), `app/models/comment.rb:232` (`calculated_confidence`).

Single call site to preserve the contract: `app/models/vote.rb:181`. Other callers that invoke it with `(0,0)` to force a DB recompute (must still pass after rework): `app/models/comment.rb:484`, `app/models/comment.rb:543`, `app/models/hidden_story.rb:15`, `app/models/hidden_story.rb:21`, `app/models/story.rb:868`.

Specs that must cover the recalculation:
- `spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82` (context "upvoting a story and flags" — all 6 vote-transition cases asserting `score`/`flags`), plus `:93` (story upvote + karma), `:109`, `:124` (hide vote no-op), `:137`, `:162`, and the comment cases `:144-179`.
- `spec/models/story_spec.rb:404-429` (describe "update_score_and_recalculate") and `spec/models/story_spec.rb:373-401` (describe "#calculated_hotness").
- `spec/models/comment_spec.rb:226-242` (describe "confidence") and `spec/models/comment_spec.rb:244` (describe "confidence_order_path"), plus comment-flag paths at `:141`, `:188`, `:201`, `:215`.

```json

  "step1_locate": {
    "shared_method": "Vote.vote_thusly_on_story_or_comment_for_user_because @ app/models/vote.rb:127",
    "recalculation_call_site": "app/models/vote.rb:181 (t.update_score_and_recalculate!(score_delta, flag_delta))",
    "target_resolution": "app/models/vote.rb:180 (t = v.target); Vote#target @ app/models/vote.rb:185-191 -> Comment.find(comment_id) or Story.find(story_id)"
  },
  "step2_recalculation": {
    "story": {
      "method": "Story#update_score_and_recalculate! @ app/models/story.rb:693-718",
      "in_memory_mutated": ["self.score @ 694", "self.flags @ 695"],
      "columns_written": ["score", "flags", "hotness"],
      "value_method": "calculated_hotness @ app/models/story.rb:550"
    },
    "comment": {
      "method": "Comment#update_score_and_recalculate! @ app/models/comment.rb:339-363",
      "in_memory_mutated": ["self.score @ 340", "self.flags @ 341"],
      "columns_written": ["score", "flags", "confidence", "confidence_order"],
      "value_method": "calculated_confidence @ app/models/comment.rb:232"
    }
  },
  "step3_map": {
    "edit": [
      "app/models/story.rb:693-718",
      "app/models/comment.rb:339-363",
      "app/models/story.rb:550 (calculated_hotness, if scoring changes)",
      "app/models/comment.rb:232 (calculated_confidence, if scoring changes)"
    ],
    "call_site": "app/models/vote.rb:181",
    "other_callers_passing_0_0": [
      "app/models/comment.rb:484",
      "app/models/comment.rb:543",
      "app/models/hidden_story.rb:15",
      "app/models/hidden_story.rb:21",
      "app/models/story.rb:868"
    ],
    "specs": [
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:93",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:109",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:124",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:137",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:162",
      "spec/models/vote_spec.rb:144-179",
      "spec/models/story_spec.rb:404-429",
      "spec/models/story_spec.rb:373-401",
      "spec/models/comment_spec.rb:226-242",
      "spec/models/comment_spec.rb:244",

      "spec/models/comment_spec.rb:188",
```
